content.text: "The Intercompany Personnel Bureau reminds all contractors that species-based discrimination during team formation is prohibited under Section 7 of the Personhood Accords. The Bureau also notes that 'prohibited' and 'enforced' are distinct concepts, and that the gap between them is measured in centuries of institutional inertia. Please select your team members based on capability, not biology. This is mandatory guidance that nobody checks."

Arcane Steel features seven playable species. All use the same stat array, kits, augment trees, and progression system. Species provides flavor, roleplay hooks, and minor mechanical adjustments — not power spikes. A Human Berserker and a Keth Berserker use the same tree. The Keth just has four arms while doing it.

content.label: The Design Law
content.text: Species adjustments are small and lateral — verbs, economies, and funnel adjustments, never raw stat bonuses. Every benefit is paired with a cost in the same currency family, plus a paperwork line: in a corp world, biology is bureaucracy.

rows: 👤 Human
Engineered ascent — extra LP from study, a friendlier training funnel
Magically intolerant — the smallest free vent of any species
🌿 Vael
The crest — automatic rift sense, sharper squad intel; bloom blood — easier venting, slower Charge; photosynthesis
Cards face-up — deception never profits; communal biology — isolation is damage
⛏ Keth
Four arms and native Titan Grip; crystalline skin, infrared, stone gut
Dense and deliberate — mass taxes movement and Evasion; custom-fitted armor; the silence
🦴 Grunt
Regeneration — and the only built-in Saturation detox; the long think
Unhurried — never first-mover; the frame — light armor doesn't fit
👁 Whisper
Dimensional perception — free Analyze against rift phenomena; the adjustment
The wrongness with strangers; never on the books — licensed goods cost extra
⚡ Flicker
1.5× nerve speed — priority and the fastest hands alive
Fragile, burning hot, small frame
🔮 Hollow
Pattern engine — reads deception, immune to fear's Rattled; tech-lane repair at full value
No spirit in the shape; medicine-lane healing works worse

content.text: Each species' page owns its numbers — the table above is the shape of the trade, not the rules text. Every species also carries a paperwork line; it's printed where the rest of its mechanics live.

Humans dominate — roughly 70% of the global population, 100% of corporate governance. The other six species make up the remaining 30%, and that 30% does the work that keeps the world running. Keth mine the materials. Vael tend the biomes. Grunts hold the lines. Flickers move the data. Whispers warn about the rifts. Hollows process the patterns. Humans sign the contracts and keep the profits.

In corporate territories, species determines your opportunities. In the contractor margins, species determines your role. The margin is the closest thing to equality the world offers — not because contractors are enlightened, but because they can't afford to be prejudiced. When your crew is one bad contract from dying, you take the Keth mechanic and the Flicker medic and you don't ask where they came from.

content.label: Mixed Species
content.text: Rift genetics are strangely compatible where magic is involved — a sentence that raises more questions than it answers. Mixed-species characters exist and can be played. Combine two species' lines with the GM, keeping the benefit/cost budget level — the design law above is the budget. Mixed-heritage characters face unique social pressures: too human for non-human communities, too alien for corporate society, perfectly suited for the margins where nobody fits in anyway.

The legal framework that recognizes non-human sapient species as persons. Signed in 2638, after three uprisings forced the corporations' hand. The Accords guarantee freedom from involuntary servitude, property rights, legal representation, and equal compensation. On paper. In practice, compliance is self-reported by the corporations, enforcement bodies don't exist, and 'equal' is a word flexible enough to cover almost anything.
For detailed species lore, history, political organizations, and inter-species dynamics, see the World section pages on Non-Human Species, Species History, and Non-Human Communities.
Seven species. One world. Zero seats at the table for six of them. The math hasn't changed.
